lines.text: Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is sitting in the canoe as Joey runs through the door carrying an outdoor patio table.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!! We are so in luck! Treeger said that we could have all this cool stuff from the basement. Wait right there. (Goes back into the hall)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, I'm, I'm paddling away!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Returning carrying a couple of rusted lawn chairs) Huh?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! Really?! We get all this rusty crap for free?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-huh. This and a bunch of bubble wrap. And, some of it is not even popped!
They both sit down at the table and the chick and the duck enter from Joey's bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Could we be more white trash?
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Monica, Ross, Phoebe, and Rachel are eating breakfast.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Entering from her bedroom) How desperate am I?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Good thing Chandler's not here, he always wins at this game.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just told my Mom I'd cater a party for her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because I need the money, and I thought that it'd be a great way to get rid of that last little schmidgen of self-respect.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, I think this is a good thing. I don't think Mom would've hired you if she didn't think you were good at what you do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don't have to stick up for her. She can't here you.
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is reading the paper and Chandler is getting ready for work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey! Umm, do you guys have any juice?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Just pickle.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey uh, Rach, funny story. I ah, bumped into Joanna on the street yesterday.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My boss, Joanna? Wow, that must've been awkward.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, no, actually she uh, asked me if I wanted to get a drink.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) You ah, you didn't say 'Yes' to that did you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (laughs) No. No!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (Coming out of the shower wearing nothing but a towel) Hello, Rachel. (She goes into Chandler's bedroom)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, not at first.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What is she doing here?
Joey makes a sound like a creaking bed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't understand! Last time you went out with her you said she was a 'big, dull dud.'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I think I judged her too quickly, and this time we were able to take the relationship to the next level.
Joey creaks louder

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, last time I almost got fired. You must end it, you must end it now!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, come on! It's not like this is an everyday occurrence for me! I mean usually I'm pretty much just in there by myself.
Joey makes a sound imitating one person making a bed creak and Chandler turns and glares at him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler!! (He turns around quickly) Promise me, you will end it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I promise, I'll end it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I hope you know what I'm giving up for ya, because she's not just the boss in your office, if you know what I mean.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-eh-eah! (Rachel glares at him) Oh-oh, sorry, I-I knew what he meant.
The Geller household kitchen, Monica and Phoebe are cooking for Mrs. Geller's party.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (entering) How's the hired help?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Doing great, the quiches are coming along.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: What's this? Blue nail polish?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I thought it was cute.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Ahh, that's what your Grandmother's hands looked like when we found her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let me ask you a question.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why did you hire me?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh, well Richard raved about the food at his party, of course you were sleeping with him. Then I heard the food at that lesbian wedding was very nice, I assume you weren't sleeping with anybody there. Though, at least that would be something. (Leaves)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Oh my God! Did you hear that? She hired me because she thinks I'm good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I didn't hear that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah, she didn't hire me out of pity, it wasn't so she could pick on me in front of her friends, she actually thinks I'm good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! And hey, it's cool if you're a lesbian! (Gives her a thumbs up)
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is scrapping gum off the table as there is a knock on the door. He goes over and opens it.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: (Entering before Joey can say anything) Good afternoon, are you the decision maker of the house?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhhhh. (He's not sure)

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Do you ah, currently own a set of encyclopedias?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No. But ah, try the classifieds, people sell everything in there.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Actually, I'm not buying. I'm selling. Let me ask you one question. Do your friends ever have a conversation and you just nod along even though you're not really sure what they're talking about?
We go into a flashback sequence with Joey remembering some of those times.
[Cut to Monica and Rachel's apartment, all are there.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...I'm telling you it's totally unconstituional.

lines.speaker: Monica, Chandler, Phoebe, and Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, I totally agree.
Joey just nods his head.
[Cut to Central Perk, the entire gang is there.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ...I think he deserves a Nobel Prize. (Joey starts to nod 'Yes.')

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Nooo!! (Joey quickly stops nodding his head.)
[Cut to Monica and Rachel's, they're all there playing cards.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...it was like the Algonquin kids table. (They all laugh, but Joey only laughs not to be left out.)
[Cut back to the present day.]

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: (Interrupting the flashback) Excuse me, I'm sorry, you haven't said anything for about two and a half minutes, are you at all interested?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-well-yeah! Yeah-oh-yeah. Come on in.
The Geller's Kitchen, Monica and Phoebe are still cooking.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's weird.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Your nails.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I know, I never wear fake ones. I just did it so my Mom wouldn't give me grief about me biting them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, no, I meant that it's weird that you only have nine now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Looks at her nails) Oh my God. Wait a minute, I had them put... (realises) Oh my God! It's in the quiche! Oh My God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, don't panic. I'm gonna go to the store, I'm gonna get you another set of nails, no one's gonna know, and you're gonna look great. (She runs over to get her coat.) Oh! Oh, it's 'cause they're gonna eat-that's the problem.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (entering) (to Monica) Honey, don't bite your nails.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay ah, please don't freak out. Umm, but ah, there's a blue fingernail in one of the quiche cups, and there's no way to know which one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And! Whoever finds it wins the prize!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (laughs) I'm not freaking out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then why are you laughing?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: It's nothing, it's just that now your Father owes me five dollars.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? You bet I'd lose a nail?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh no, don't be silly. I just bet I'd need these. (Opens the freezer to reveal...)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Frozen lasagnas?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You bet that I'd screw up?! So all that stuff about hiring me because I was good was...

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: No-no-no, that was all true. This was just in case you pulled a Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You promised Dr. Weinburg, you'd never use that phrase.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh honey, come on, have a sense of humour, you've never been able to laugh at yourself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs) That's right. My Mom doesn't have any faith in me! Oh, that's hilarious! Ha-ha-ha-ha-ha.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't get it.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: No, I have faith...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (interrupting) No! You have lasagnas! (Storms out and an awkward silence follows.)

lines.speaker: Oven
lines.text: Ding!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Op, the ruined quiches are ready.
Joanna's office, Joanna and Chandler are making out on her chair. Chandler isn't wearing any pants.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It just doesn't...feel like we're breaking up.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: No, we are. I'm sad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
They start kissing again, but are interrupted by the phone.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (answering the phone) Yes. (listens) Uh, can't you wait until tomorrow? (listens) All right. (hangs up) Unbelievable!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: No, no, that was my boss. I have to go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (Starts to button up his shirt)

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm getting dressed.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When I walk outside naked people throw garbage at me.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Wait. I wanna show you something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Just a little gag gift somebody gave me. (She's holding a pair of handcuffs) Put your hands together.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah-ha, you're not the boss of me. (She kisses him) Yeah, you are! (She handcuffs him to the chair) Ooh, saucy.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (kisses him) I'll be back in ten minutes. (Starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You are, you're gonna leave me like this?

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Knowing you're here, waiting for me I think it's kinda exciting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. But if you don't come back soon, (She leaves and closes the door) there's pretty much nothing I can do about it!
[Cut to Joanna's outer office, where Rachel and Sophie work. They are both coming back from lunch.]

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (locking her door) Oh.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Hi! I brought you back a macaroon!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh great! I'll keep it in my butt with your nose. (She grabs the cookie and walks out.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's weird, she locked the door.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Y'know why? She's got the Christmas bonus list in there. I saw her working on it this morning.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, swear you won't tell, but when Mark left he gave me a key to Joanna's office. Do you wanna see the list?

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Yeah!
Rachel unlocks and opens the door to reveal a half-naked Chandler handcuffed to the chair. They both gasp and Chandler stares at them in shock and surprise.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi! (to Sophie) How are you?
Rachel and Sophie both back out and close the door without saying anything.
Commercial Break
Joanna's office, Chandler, still handcuffed to the chair, is looking through the lingerie catalogue by turning the pages with his teeth. The phone rings and Chandler answers it with his nose.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello, Joanna...(Realises he doesn't know her last name)...'s office.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (on speaker phone) I'm really sorry but I may be a little while longer.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How little?!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: A couple of hours, I feel awful.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, this isn't funny! You get back here right now!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: I can't!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: I'm in my boss's car!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Uh-oh, tunnel. (The phone gets cut off)
Chandler gets an idea
[Cut to Rachel's office as her intercom buzzes.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (answering it) (angrily) What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (in a serious, businesslike tone) Rachel, could I see you for a moment?
Rachel goes into talk to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here's the situation. The keys to the cuffs are on the back of the door. Could you be a doll and grab them and scoot on over and unlock me? And on a totally different subject, that is a lovely pantsuit.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You promised you would break up with her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I did break up with her! She just took it really, really well!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And the fact that you were jeopardising my career never entered your mind?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It did enter my mind! But then something happened that made it, shoot right out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know what Chandler, you got yourself into those cuffs, you get yourself out of them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no-no-no-no!! I can't get myself right out of them! You must have me confused with the Amazing Chandler!! Come on, you have to unlock me, she could be gone for hours, and I'm cold, and (Stops and looks up the skirt on a statue behind Joanna's desk.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Chandler!! All right, this is it! (Grabs the key) You never see Joanna again!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Never!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You never come into this office again!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You give me back my Walkman!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I-never borrowed your Walkman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, then I lost it. You buy me one!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You got it! Here we go! Come on! This is great! (Rachel goes over and unlocks the handcuffs) Ahhh! (He starts rubbing his wrist)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Does it hurt?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I just always see guys doing this when they get handcuffs taken off them. (He runs over to where his pants are hanging) Hello sweet pants!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait a minute! What are you gonna tell Joanna?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: About what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: When she sees that you're gone, she's gonna know that I let you out, and that I was in here, and I'm gonna get fired!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll make something up! I'm good at lying, I actually did borrow your Walkman!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, there's nothing to make up, she's gonna know that I have a key to her office, I've got to get you locked up back the way you were! (She tries to drag him over to the chair, but Chandler stops her.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-ho-ho, I don't think so!

He starts to put his pants on, but Rachel manages to drag him to the chair. When they get to the chair, Chandler drops his pants and knocks the chair away. Rachel then backs him up and locks him to the top drawer of a filing cabinet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, this is much better.
Chandler and Joey's, The salesman is trying to sell Joey the encyclopedias.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: So, here's somebody interesting, Joey. What do you know about Van Gogh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: He cut off his ear.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm out.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: He painted that. (Points to one of his paintings in the book)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! That's pretty nice. I thought he cut off his ear 'cause he sucked. What else you got in there?

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Let's see, ahhh... Where does the Pope live?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: In the woods. No wait-wait, that's the joke answer.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Actually its, Vatican City. Now ahh, what do you know about vulcanised rubber?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Spock's birth control.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: (laughs) You need these books.
Monica's childhood bedroom (which has been turned into a gym), Monica is lying on the treadmill as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This used to be your room? (She nods 'Yes') Wow! You must've been in really good shape as a kid.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, I'm such an idiot. I can't believe I actually thought she could change.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, who cares what your Mom thinks? So you pulled a Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good, I'm glad that's catching on.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No but, why does that have to be a bad thing. Just change what it means. Y'know? Go down there and prove your Mother wrong. Finish the job you were hired to do, and we'll call that pulling a Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, umm, if a kid gets straight A's, his parents would say, "Yeah, he pulled a Monica." Y'know? Or a fireman saves a baby, and they go, "Yeah I know, he pulled a Monica." Or someone hits a homerun and the announcer says, "Yeah, that one's outta here." Though some things don't change.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (getting up) All right, I'll go down there. But, I'm not gonna serve the lasagna. I'm gonna serve something I make.
She exits and Phoebe goes over and sits down at the machine that works your shoulders and tries to do one, which she does, easily.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! My breasts are really strong. (She goes and joins Monica.)
Joanna's office, Rachel and Chandler are having a little tug-of-war with his pants.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler! Chandler, please, I have to get you locked up back the way you were, I am sooo gonna lose my job, she's very private about her office. Now I know why.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, look, you're in trouble either way! Okay? If she comes back and sees me locked to this instead of the chair, she's gonna know you were in here. So you might as well just let me go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What if I clean your bathroom for a month?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It still wouldn't be clean. (Rachel makes an 'Eww, disgusting!' face) All I want is my freedom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Foot rubs for a month!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Freedom!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'll take all of your photos and put them into photo albums!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Freedom! I want my freedom! Why won't you here me?! (Opens the door) Sophie, help me! Help me!! (Sophie stands up)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sophie sit!!
She closes the door and puts his tie into his mouth as a gag.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! God, would you just calm down!
Chandler screams a little bit, then realises that he can spit out his gag. He does so with a 'Pouff!'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm gonna say this for the last time. Would you please just... (He moves his arm which opens the drawer and hits in the back of the head, which proves his point.)
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is now reading the 'V' book, with the salesman watching.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! There's a lot I didn't know about vomit. (The duck comes to the door of the bathroom, quacking.) (To the duck) In a minute. (The duck goes back into the bathroom.)

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: So, what do you say, Joey? You get the whole set of encyclopedias for twelve hundred dollars, which works out to just 50 bucks a book!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Twelve hundred dollars? You think I have $1200? I'm home in the middle of the day, and I got patio furniture in my living room. I guess there's a few things you don't get from book learnin'.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Well ah, what can you swing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How about zero down and zero a month for a long, long time?

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: You don't have, anything?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You wanna see what I got? (He gets up to empty out his pockets) Okay? I've got a baby Tootsie Roll, a movie stub, keys, a Kleenex, a rock, and an army man. Hey!

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Okay, I-I get the picture. Uh, thanks, for your time. (Starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And a 50. (The salesman stops suddenly) Huh, these must be Chandler's pants.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: For 50 bucks, you can get one book! What will it be? A? B? C?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I-I think I'm gonna stick with the V, I wanna see how this bad boy turns out.
Joanna's office, Rachel and Chandler are still negotiating.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I ah, will buy and wrap all of your Christmas gifts.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I ah... Oh! I'll squeeze you fresh orange juice every morning!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: With extra pulp?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (happily) Yeah!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: D'oh!! (pause) I've got it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't have it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I have so got it. There's gonna be rumours about this, there's no way to stop it. Sophie knows, Monica and Phoebe know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How do Monica and Phoebe know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I called them. And when they ask me what I saw, I can be very generous (Holds her hands far apart) or very (In a high pitched voice) stingy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (intrigued) Go on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can make you a legend. I can make you this generation's Milton Berle.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And Milton Berle has a...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, not compared to you. (Chandler nods in agreement)
The Geller's kitchen, Phoebe is bringing in some dirty dishes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They're not even touching the lasagna!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, they love your casserole.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's hard to believe that just a little while ago this was nothing but ingredients.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (entering) Well, everyone seems to be enjoying your dish.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And you?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I thought it was... quite tasty.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So if everyone liked it, and you liked it, that would make this a success. Which would make you...

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (interrupting) A bitch?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I was going for wrong, but we can use your word.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Yes, well I was wrong, and I have to say you really impressed me today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, you might even say that she pulled a Monica. (They both look at her) (to Monica) She doesn't know we switched it. (Monica nods her head 'No.')

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: And the next time you cater for me, there will be nothing but ice in the freezer. (She starts to bite her nails)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That really means a lot. Oh, and Mom, don't bite your nails.
Central Perk, all except Chandler, are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (hello) Hello.

lines.speaker: Joey, Rachel, and Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (in a sexy voice) Hello, Chandler. (Phoebe has a huge smile on her face.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Rachel) I love you. (Kisses her on the forehead)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wh-what's going on?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.
She motions for them to come closer, they lean in and she whispers what Rachel told her. The guys both lean back laughing.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No he doesn't!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (checks his watch) Two hours, that lasted!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So did you break up with Joanna?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, it's good thing you got out when you did, before she blew up like that Vesuvius.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The volcano?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. And speaking of volcanoes, man are they a violent igneous rock formation.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, lava spewing, hot ash, of course some are dormant.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why are you talking about volcanoes all of the sudden?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, we can talk about something else. What do you want to talk about? Vivisection? The Vasdeferens? The Vietnam War?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! Did anybody see that-that documentary on the Korean War? (Joey is pissed)

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh, yeah. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh God, Korea is such a beautiful country.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: With such a sad history.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Could there be more Kims?
They all laugh and Joey joins them, not to be left out. When the laughing dies down, he has a depressed look on his face.
Closing Credits
Rachel's office, Rachel is coming in for the day.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (from her office) Who's out there?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's me! Good morning!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Rachel, could you come in here for a moment, please?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, sure. Umm, they didn't have poppy seed bagels, so I... (Enters Joanna's office and sees her handcuffed to her chair wearing nothing but a slip) Oh my word!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: I seem to have had a slight office mishap. Could you please get the key off the back of the door for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah! Yeah!
She goes back and forth, not sure what to do first, put the bagel down or grab the key. She finally puts the bagel down and grabs the key and goes over to unlock Joanna.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: You tell your friend Chandler that we're definately broken up this time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.
End
